sections.about: This metric measures the number of women directors on the Board. It falls under BRSR Section A — General Disclosures, Participation/Inclusion/Representation of women; it is not an Essential or Leadership Indicator. A higher value indicates better performance.
sections.performanceAndDrivers: Jindal Stainless is consistently strong on absolute women directors, with 3 in each year; JSW also ends FY2024-25 at 3 after dipping to 2 in FY2023-24. Tata had 2 women directors in FY2022-23 and FY2023-24, falling to 1 in FY2024-25. SAIL fell from 3 to 2 to 0, creating a significant governance-diversity gap in the latest year. Tata’s FY2024-25 BRSR explains that Ms. Farida Khambata ceased to be an Independent Director effective December 11, 2024 after completion of her term, which directly explains Tata’s reduction in women directors.
sections.tataPositioning: In FY2024-25, Tata ranks 3rd with 1 woman director, 66.7% below JSW and Jindal Stainless, both at 3. Tata improved in rank from 4th in FY2022-23 to tied 2nd in FY2023-24 because peers converged, but declined to rank 3 in FY2024-25 after the reduction from 2 to 1 female director. The driver is Board succession timing rather than operating performance; however, from a senior governance lens, it is a visible deterioration.
